annotations.label: Exponential function (lead definition)

anchor.snippet: the unique real function which maps zero to one

annotations.note: Real.exp is the real exponential function (defined via Complex.exp), with Real.exp_zero giving exp 0 = 1.

annotations.label: Converts sums to products

anchor.snippet: The exponential function converts sums to products

annotations.note: Real.exp_add states exp (x + y) = exp x * exp y.

annotations.label: Faster than every power

anchor.snippet: is upward-sloping, and increases faster than every power of

annotations.note: Real.tendsto_exp_div_pow_atTop shows exp x / x^n → ∞, i.e. exp outgrows every power.

annotations.label: Horizontal asymptote

anchor.snippet: The graph always lies above the x -axis

annotations.note: Real.exp_pos gives 0 < exp x (graph above the x-axis); Real.tendsto_exp_atBot gives the asymptote at 0.

annotations.label: Slope equals height

anchor.snippet: the slope of the tangent to the graph at each point is equal to its height

annotations.note: Real.hasDerivAt_exp states HasDerivAt exp (exp x) x, so the slope equals the value.

annotations.label: Differential equation definition

anchor.snippet: the unique differentiable function that equals its derivative

annotations.note: Real.deriv_exp shows exp' = exp, but the uniqueness characterization (exp as THE solution with f 0 = 1) is not formalized as a definition.

annotations.label: Inverse of natural logarithm

anchor.snippet: is the inverse function of the natural logarithm

annotations.note: Real.exp_log (exp (log x) = x for x > 0) and Real.log_exp (log (exp x) = x) establish the inverse relationship.

annotations.label: Power series definition

anchor.snippet: is the sum of the power series

annotations.note: Complex.exp (and hence Real.exp) is defined as CauSeq.lim of the partial sums ∑ xᵐ/m!, i.e. the sum of its Taylor series.

anchors.snippet: is the sum of the power series

annotations.label: Absolute convergence of series

anchor.snippet: This series is absolutely convergent for every

annotations.note: Complex.isCauSeq_norm_exp shows the series of norms ∑ ‖zᵐ/m!‖ is Cauchy, i.e. the exponential series is absolutely convergent.

annotations.label: Functional equation

anchor.snippet: and maps the additive identity 0 to the multiplicative identity 1

annotations.note: Real.exp_add gives exp(x+y)=exp x·exp y and Real.exp_zero gives exp 0 = 1, the functional equation.

anchors.snippet: and maps the additive identity 0 to the multiplicative identity 1

annotations.label: Characterization by derivative at 0

anchor.snippet: characterized by the property that its derivative at 0 is 1

annotations.note: The fact exp'(0)=1 follows from Real.hasDerivAt_exp and exp 0 = 1, but the uniqueness characterization among functional-equation solutions is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: characterized by the property that its derivative at 0 is 1

annotations.label: Limit of integer powers

anchor.snippet: is the limit , as the integer n goes to infinity

annotations.note: Real.tendsto_one_add_div_pow_exp states (1 + t/n)^n → exp t as n → ∞.

anchors.snippet: is the limit , as the integer n goes to infinity

annotations.label: Reciprocal property

anchor.snippet: The functional equation implies

annotations.note: Real.exp_neg states exp (-x) = (exp x)⁻¹.

anchors.snippet: The functional equation implies

annotations.label: Positiveness

anchor.snippet: Positiveness

annotations.note: Real.exp_pos states 0 < exp x for all real x.

annotations.label: Monotonically increasing

anchor.snippet: the exponential function is monotonically increasing

annotations.note: Real.exp_strictMono states StrictMono exp.

annotations.label: Exponentiation with positive real base

anchor.snippet: Extension of exponentiation to positive real bases

annotations.note: Real.rpow defines x^y = exp (y * log x) for positive base, extending exponentiation to real exponents.

annotations.label: Exponential function (indefinite article)

anchor.snippet: A function is commonly called an exponential function

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general predicate/definition for what counts as 'an exponential function' of the form a·bˣ.

annotations.label: General-form exponential function

anchor.snippet: the term exponential function is commonly used for functions of the form

annotations.note: No Mathlib definition packages functions of the form a·bˣ as a named class.

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations

anchor.snippet: the differentiable functions that satisfy the following equivalent characterizations

annotations.note: The equivalence of the various characterizations of general exponential functions is not formalized as a theorem.

annotations.label: Base of an exponential function

anchor.snippet: The base of an exponential function is the base of the exponentiation

annotations.note: No Mathlib notion of 'the base' of an exponential function exists.

annotations.label: Exponential growth and decay models

anchor.snippet: are thus modeled with exponential functions

annotations.note: Applied exponential growth/decay modeling is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decay/rate constant

anchor.snippet: is called, depending on the context, the decay constant

annotations.note: The notion of a decay/rate constant is application terminology not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence of first two characterizations

anchor.snippet: The two first characterizations are equivalent

annotations.note: The equivalence of the functional-equation and differential-equation characterizations is not formalized as a stated theorem.

anchors.snippet: The two first characterizations are equivalent

annotations.label: Continuously compounded interest

anchor.snippet: If a principal amount of 1 earns interest at an annual rate of x compounded monthly

annotations.note: The compound-interest application example is not formalized, though the underlying limit (1+x/n)^n→exp x is (Real.tendsto_one_add_div_pow_exp).

anchors.snippet: If a principal amount of 1 earns interest at an annual rate of x compounded monthly

annotations.label: Solution of simplest differential equation

anchor.snippet: is a solution of the simplest possible differential equation

annotations.note: Real.hasDerivAt_exp shows exp solves y' = y.

annotations.label: All solutions are exponential

anchor.snippet: and every solution of this differential equation has this form

annotations.note: A theorem characterizing all solutions of y' = y as C·exp x was not found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solutions of forced equation

anchor.snippet: involve exponential functions in a more sophisticated way

annotations.note: Solution formulas for forced linear ODEs y' = y + f via exponentials are not formalized as such.

anchors.snippet: involve exponential functions in a more sophisticated way

annotations.label: Linear constant-coefficient solutions

anchor.snippet: can be expressed in terms of exponential functions

annotations.note: General solution of linear constant-coefficient ODEs in terms of exponentials is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex exponential extension

anchor.snippet: can be naturally extended to a complex function

annotations.note: Complex.exp extends the real exponential, with Complex.ofReal_exp showing (Real.exp x : ℂ) = Complex.exp x.

annotations.label: Complex exponential by derivative

anchor.snippet: the unique complex function that equals its complex derivative

annotations.note: Complex.hasDerivAt_exp shows exp' = exp, but the uniqueness characterization by the derivative condition is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: the unique complex function that equals its complex derivative

annotations.label: Complex exponential series

anchor.snippet: is the sum of the series

annotations.note: Complex.exp is defined as the limit of partial sums ∑ zᵐ/m! (Complex.exp', isCauSeq_exp).

anchors.snippet: is the sum of the series

annotations.label: Complex exponential is entire

anchor.snippet: the complex exponential is an entire function

annotations.note: Complex.analyticOnNhd_cexp shows exp is analytic on all of ℂ (also Complex.differentiable_exp), i.e. entire.

anchors.snippet: the complex exponential is an entire function

annotations.label: Complex exponential as limit

anchor.snippet: The complex exponential function is the limit

annotations.note: Complex.tendsto_one_add_div_pow_exp states (1 + t/n)^n → exp t for t : ℂ.

anchors.snippet: The complex exponential function is the limit

annotations.label: Complex functional equation

anchor.snippet: the complex exponential satisfies the functional equation

annotations.note: Complex.exp_add states exp (x + y) = exp x * exp y over ℂ.

anchors.snippet: the complex exponential satisfies the functional equation

annotations.label: Uniqueness via holomorphy

anchor.snippet: the unique solution which is holomorphic at the point

annotations.note: No uniqueness theorem characterizing exp as the unique holomorphic solution of f' = f was found in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the unique solution which is holomorphic at the point

annotations.label: Complex logarithm right-inverse

anchor.snippet: is a right-inverse function of the complex exponential

annotations.note: Complex.exp_log states exp (log z) = z for z ≠ 0, so log is a right inverse of exp.

anchors.snippet: is a right-inverse function of the complex exponential

annotations.label: Periodicity of complex exponential

anchor.snippet: It is periodic function of period

annotations.note: Complex.exp_periodic states Function.Periodic exp (2 * π * I).

anchors.snippet: It is periodic function of period

annotations.label: Complex conjugate of exponential

anchor.snippet: The complex conjugate of the complex exponential is

annotations.note: Complex.exp_conj states exp (conj x) = conj (exp x).

anchors.snippet: The complex conjugate of the complex exponential is

annotations.label: Modulus of complex exponential

anchor.snippet: Its modulus is

annotations.note: Complex.norm_exp states ‖exp z‖ = Real.exp z.re.

anchors.snippet: Its modulus is

annotations.label: Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: are strongly related by Euler's formula

annotations.note: Complex.exp_mul_I states exp (x * I) = cos x + sin x * I, Euler's formula.

anchors.snippet: are strongly related by Euler's formula

annotations.label: Real and imaginary decomposition

anchor.snippet: the decomposition of complex exponentials into real and imaginary parts

annotations.note: Complex.exp_eq_exp_re_mul_sin_add_cos (with Complex.exp_re/exp_im) decomposes exp z into real-exp times cos/sin of the imaginary part.

anchors.snippet: the decomposition of complex exponentials into real and imaginary parts

annotations.label: Trig functions via exponentials

anchor.snippet: can be expressed in terms of complex exponentials

annotations.note: Complex.two_cos (2 cos x = exp(xI) + exp(-xI)) and Complex.two_sin express cos/sin via exponentials.

anchors.snippet: can be expressed in terms of complex exponentials

annotations.label: Exponential is transcendental function

anchor.snippet: is a transcendental function

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize that exp is a transcendental function (not algebraic over the field of rational functions).

annotations.label: Linear independence of exponentials

anchor.snippet: are linearly independent over

annotations.note: linearIndependent_monoidHom (Artin's linear independence of distinct characters) generalizes the linear independence of exponential functions, but the specific exp statement is not stated.

annotations.label: e is transcendental (Lindemann–Weierstrass)

anchor.snippet: is a transcendental number

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass (exp_polynomial_approx) is in Mathlib; the transcendence of e itself is not yet proven.

annotations.label: expm1 routine

anchor.snippet: which computes e x − 1 directly

annotations.note: There is no Real.expm1 definition; Mathlib only has bounds like Complex.norm_exp_sub_one_le, not a dedicated eˣ−1 routine.

anchors.snippet: which computes e x − 1 directly

annotations.label: Hyperbolic tangent identity

anchor.snippet: An identity in terms of the hyperbolic tangent

annotations.note: The identity eˣ = (1+tanh(x/2))/(1−tanh(x/2)) is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: An identity in terms of the hyperbolic tangent

annotations.label: Continued fraction via Euler identity

anchor.snippet: can be obtained via an identity of Euler

annotations.note: The continued-fraction expansion of eˣ via Euler's identity is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: can be obtained via an identity of Euler

annotations.label: Faster-converging continued fraction

anchor.snippet: converges more quickly

annotations.note: The faster-converging continued fraction for eˣ is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: converges more quickly

annotations.label: Exponential on Banach algebras

anchor.snippet: makes sense for square matrices

annotations.note: NormedSpace.exp defines the exponential on any Banach algebra via the series ∑ xⁿ/n!, specializing to Matrix.exp.

annotations.label: Invertibility in Banach algebra

anchor.snippet: is invertible with inverse

annotations.note: NormedSpace.isUnit_exp shows exp x is a unit and Ring.inverse_exp gives (exp x)⁻¹ = exp (-x).

annotations.label: Failure of addition law for noncommuting

anchor.snippet: but this identity can fail for noncommuting x and y

annotations.note: NormedSpace.exp_add_of_commute proves exp(x+y)=exp x·exp y only under a Commute hypothesis, but the explicit failure for noncommuting elements is not a proven counterexample.

annotations.label: Alternative limit definition

anchor.snippet: Some alternative definitions lead to the same function

annotations.note: An (1 + x/n)ⁿ-type limit definition agreeing with NormedSpace.exp on general Banach algebras was not found.

anchors.snippet: Some alternative definitions lead to the same function

annotations.label: Definition via differential equation

anchor.snippet: is the solution to the differential equation

annotations.note: A characterization of the matrix/Banach exponential as the solution of X' = AX is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie algebra exponential map

anchor.snippet: the exponential map is a map

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general Lie group/Lie algebra exponential map.

annotations.label: Real exponential as Lie special case

anchor.snippet: is a special case of the Lie algebra situation

annotations.note: Real.exp exists, but there is no Lie group exponential map for it to be presented as a special case of.

annotations.label: Matrix exponential as Lie special case

anchor.snippet: is a special case of the Lie algebra exponential map

annotations.note: The matrix exponential (NormedSpace.exp on Matrix n n 𝔸, with Matrix.exp_* lemmas) exists, but no Lie algebra exponential map frames it as a special case.

annotations.label: Baker–Campbell–Hausdorff correction

anchor.snippet: can fail for Lie algebra elements x and y that do not commute

annotations.note: The Baker–Campbell–Hausdorff formula/correction is not formalized in Mathlib.
